source.catalog_ref: I-EU13 (IGVC 2026, Latin America + Canada + Nordic + ESA subsection)
source.multipart_basis: Design-specific decomposition of the IGVC 2026 AGV: a welded 1018 CR steel chassis plate with side rails, four motor/wheel mounts, a sensor tower for LiDAR/RGB/GPS/IMU, a payload deck, a LIDAR mast, and a battery tray. Reflects the physical build required to satisfy IGVC 2026 rules (E-stop heights, 20 lb payload basket, tow point). No rulebook citations fabricated; all pass/fail thresholds preserved verbatim.

full_prompt:
Design the primary chassis frame of an IGVC 2026 autonomous ground vehicle per IGVC 2026 Rules. The vehicle must be between 3 and 7 ft long, 2 and 4 ft wide, 3 and 7 ft tall, with ground clearance at least 3 inches, mechanical top speed limited to 5 mph (2.24 m/s) governed, a 20 lb (9.07 kg) payload basket centered in the vehicle, a mandatory mechanical push-button E-stop on the rear between 2 and 4 ft above ground, and a wireless E-stop with minimum 100 ft range. The vehicle rolls on four wheels (differential-drive skid-steer with free castors OR 4-wheel drive) with unsprung wheel masses approximately 3 kg each and a drivetrain comprising two 24 V DC gearmotors (4 kg each) mounted to the frame. Total vehicle dry mass target 70 kg including sensors (2D LiDAR, RGB camera, GPS, IMU), compute (NVIDIA Jetson + automotive 12 V DC-DC), and a 24 V 30 Ah SLA battery pack (22 kg). Frame material: AISI 1018 CR square steel tube 25.4 mm x 25.4 mm x 2 mm wall (E 200 GPa, yield 370 MPa, ultimate 440 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3), TIG-welded. Frame footprint 900 mm x 600 mm x 600 mm tall. Load cases: (LC1) static 1 g: full 70 kg + 9.07 kg payload reacted through 4 wheel axles (vertical); (LC2) 2 g curb drop: 2 g vertical peak on 70 kg + 9.07 kg payload (IGVC vehicles routinely hop curb edges during outdoor navigation); (LC3) 1 g lateral side-slope: 1 g vertical + 1 g lateral applied at CoM while traversing 15 degree grass slope (IGVC course has slope); (LC4) 0.5 g braking at 5 mph: 0.5 g longitudinal decel + 1 g vertical; (LC5) point-load payload abuse: 500 N vertical point load concentrated at center of payload-basket floor (scrutineer test); (LC6) E-stop impact: 100 N concentrated horizontal load applied at the rear mechanical E-stop button at 1 m above ground (inspector pushes hard); (LC7) tow-point pull: 1 kN applied horizontally at a designated tow point on the front of the frame (IGVC organizers may tow a stuck vehicle off the course). Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises stress in the frame under LC1-LC5 must remain below 247 MPa (370 / 1.5 yield FoS, stated explicitly in IGVC 2026 Rules Section IX-D-4 deliverable); (R2) under LC6 and LC7, local stress must remain below yield 370 MPa, no permanent deformation; (R3) frame dry mass (tubing only, no wheels/motors/battery) must not exceed 18 kg; (R4) first frame natural frequency must exceed 30 Hz to decouple from drivetrain at 6000 RPM (100 Hz) and LiDAR scan rate (10 Hz); (R5) payload-basket floor deflection under LC5 must remain below 5 mm; (R6) ground clearance must remain at or above 76.2 mm (3 in) under LC2 2 g drop with tire deflection excluded. Thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling analyses not primary. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of frame (welded tube assembly, N tubes as separate bodies), 2D weld-map drawing, FEA per load case, modal, mass budget with component masses.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
Delivered as a multi-part assembly reflecting the physical IGVC AGV build: a welded 1018 CR chassis plate/ladder frame with two longitudinal side rails and transverse cross members, four motor/wheel mount brackets at the corners, a sensor tower rising 1-2 ft above the frame carrying RGB/IMU/GPS, a payload deck forming the 20 lb payload basket, a discrete LIDAR mast at the top of the sensor tower positioning the 2D scanner, and a battery tray containing the 22 kg SLA pack. The rear E-stop and front tow point are local features on the chassis frame. Interfaces are TIG-welded for chassis plate/side rails/cross members, bolted for motor mounts and sensor tower (to allow service access). Final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC2 2 g curb drop). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design the primary chassis frame of an IGVC 2026 autonomous ground vehicle per IGVC 2026 Rules.

Envelope: 3 to 7 ft long, 2 to 4 ft wide, 3 to 7 ft tall; ground clearance at least 3 inches; top speed governed to 5 mph (2.24 m/s); 20 lb (9.07 kg) payload basket centered; rear mechanical push-button E-stop between 2 and 4 ft above ground; wireless E-stop minimum 100 ft range. Frame footprint 900 mm x 600 mm x 600 mm tall. Four wheels (3 kg each), two 24 V DC gearmotors (4 kg each), 24 V 30 Ah SLA battery pack (22 kg); total dry mass target 70 kg.

Material: AISI 1018 CR square steel tube 25.4 mm x 25.4 mm x 2 mm wall (yield 370 MPa, ultimate 440 MPa), TIG-welded.

Load cases: (LC1) static 1 g: 70 kg + 9.07 kg through 4 wheel axles; (LC2) 2 g curb drop on 70 kg + 9.07 kg; (LC3) 1 g side-slope: 1 g vertical + 1 g lateral at CoM on 15 degree slope; (LC4) 0.5 g braking at 5 mph + 1 g vertical; (LC5) 500 N vertical point load at payload-basket floor; (LC6) E-stop impact 100 N horizontal at rear E-stop at 1 m above ground; (LC7) tow-point pull 1 kN horizontal at front.

Pass/fail: (R1) LC1-LC5 max vM < 247 MPa (370/1.5 per IGVC 2026 Rules IX-D-4); (R2) LC6/LC7 local stress < yield 370 MPa; (R3) frame dry mass (tubing) <= 18 kg; (R4) first natural frequency > 30 Hz; (R5) LC5 basket floor deflection < 5 mm; (R6) ground clearance >= 76.2 mm (3 in) under LC2.

prompt.title: IGVC 2026 Autonomous Ground Vehicle Frame (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: AISI 1018 CR welded frame for 70 kg AGV + 20 lb payload, 5 mph cap, IGVC 2026 FoS 1.5 deliverable. Decomposition: chassis plate, 2x side rails, 2-4x motor/wheel mounts, sensor tower, payload deck, LIDAR mast, battery tray. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under governing load case.

parts.name: chassis_plate
parts.role: welded 1018 CR ladder chassis plate forming the base floor that ties side rails to cross members and supports the battery tray
parts.material_hint: 1018 CR steel 3 mm plate + 25.4x25.4x2 box tubing

parts.name: side_rail
parts.role: longitudinal side rails running the 900 mm length; carry corner wheel-mount brackets and front tow point / rear E-stop boss

parts.name: motor_wheel_mount
parts.role: corner motor/wheel bracket carrying 4 kg 24 V gearmotor + 3 kg wheel; reacts LC1/LC2 vertical + LC4 braking

geometric_constraints.bolt_pattern: 4x M6 to side rail + 4x M8 to motor flange

parts.name: sensor_tower
parts.role: vertical tower rising from chassis plate carrying RGB camera, GPS, IMU, and topped with LIDAR mast; must position LIDAR per IGVC field-of-view rules

parts.name: payload_deck
parts.role: mid-height deck forming the 20 lb payload basket floor at vehicle center; reacts LC5 500 N point load within 5 mm

geometric_constraints.mount_pattern: 4x M6 to side rails and 1 cross member

parts.material_hint: 1018 CR 3 mm plate + basket perimeter tubing

parts.name: lidar_mount
parts.role: top-of-tower LIDAR mast carrying the 2D LIDAR scanner; must keep scan plane level under LC3 side slope
parts.material_hint: 1018 CR 5 mm plate or aluminum 6061-T6 5 mm

parts.name: battery_tray
parts.role: tray at floor level containing the 22 kg SLA pack; dominates CoM + LC1/LC2 inertia

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: vehicle axes: X longitudinal (front+), Y lateral, Z up; origin at chassis plate centroid on ground plane

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: 370 / 1.5 yield FoS per IGVC 2026 Rules IX-D-4

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces (welded chassis/side-rail/cross-member joints, bolted motor mounts, bolted sensor tower, bolted LIDAR mast, battery tray bolts) <= 1.0 under LC2 curb drop.
Sensor tower top (LIDAR mount) lateral displacement under LC3 side-slope must remain below 10 mm to keep the LIDAR scan plane aligned.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam (BEAM188) for tubing; shell for chassis plate and payload basket floor; solid for motor mounts

interfaces.mediated_by: TIG-welded side rails onto perimeter of chassis plate
interfaces.load_path: primary vehicle load path; reacts LC1/LC2 vertical and LC4 braking

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M6 bolted motor-wheel mount into side rail
interfaces.load_path: wheel reaction (LC1/LC2) and motor torque (LC4) from corner into rail

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M6 bolted sensor tower base into chassis plate
interfaces.load_path: sensor inertial load under LC3 side-slope and LC2 drop transferred into chassis; governs R_asm2

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M6 bolts from payload deck into side rails + 1 cross member
interfaces.load_path: LC5 500 N point-load payload reaction into rails (gates R5 deflection)

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M5 bolts from LIDAR mast into sensor tower top
6x M8 bolts from battery tray into chassis plate
interfaces.load_path: 22 kg battery inertial load (dominant mass) under LC1/LC2/LC3 reacted into chassis

interfaces.mediated_by: tow point welded boss at front of each side rail; E-stop boss welded at rear, 600 mm from ground
interfaces.load_path: LC6 E-stop push (100 N) and LC7 tow pull (1 kN) reacted into side rails

fem_check_policy.rationale: Ground-clearance gate (R6) and modal gate (R4) only exist on the assembled vehicle; LC5 payload deflection (R5) and LC7 tow-point pull require chassis + rails + payload deck + tow-point feature together; sensor tower alignment (R_asm2) only exists on the assembled stack.

notes.why_multi_part: An IGVC AGV by construction couples chassis, drivetrain, battery, sensor tower, LIDAR mast, and payload deck; rules also mandate E-stop and tow-point features that require specific frame members to react them.
notes.speculation_caveats: Counts of motor mounts (2-4, depending on 2WD vs 4WD) and cross members reflect typical IGVC practice; no new rulebook thresholds invented.
